architecture.evidence: Good module separation overall but testability concerns. Direct module imports (no DI) mean many tests require monkeypatching sys.path, module globals, and filesystem paths. The verification matrix enforces module-to-test coverage but some test files are large (test_standup.py: 77 tests). Score calculation has multiple code paths (rubric YAML vs _DEFAULT_WEIGHTS fallback) that could diverge. The state machine formalization is good for validation but transition rules are spread across pipeline_entry_state.py and advance.py.
strengths: Verification matrix enforces every module has corresponding tests
State machine formalization enables automated validation
Clean separation between live-data tests and isolated unit tests
weaknesses: No dependency injection — testing requires extensive monkeypatching
Some test files are very large, suggesting the modules they test are too coupled
Transition rules split between state module and advance script

documentation.evidence: CLAUDE.md covers testing patterns which is valuable. Test naming conventions are descriptive (test_stale_entry_flagged, test_score_out_of_range_rejected). But: no dedicated testing guide, no contribution guidelines for test writing, edge case coverage is noted in rubric but not systematically tracked. Documentation of expected failure modes is implicit in tests rather than explicit in docs.
strengths: CLAUDE.md testing patterns section guides new test authors
Test names are descriptive and follow consistent conventions
Architecture docs help understand what to test
weaknesses: No formal testing guide or contribution documentation
Edge case coverage not systematically tracked or documented

analytics_intelligence.evidence: Good analytics coverage but validation of analytics output is light. funnel_report.py produces conversion rates but no tests verify the math against known data. rejection_learner correlates outcomes but sample sizes are small and no statistical significance testing. Snapshot trend detection uses linear regression which is appropriate but no confidence intervals reported. Block-outcome classification (golden/toxic) uses fixed thresholds (50%, 75%) without rationale for those cutoffs.
strengths: Comprehensive analytics suite covering many business questions
Snapshot trend detection is methodologically sound
Rejection learning creates a feedback loop for improvement
weaknesses: Analytics output not validated by tests against known datasets
No statistical significance testing for small sample conclusions
Fixed classification thresholds lack documented rationale

sustainability.evidence: Automation is good but monitoring of automation health is reactive. launchd_manager.py checks agent status but no alerting on agent failures. Backup monitoring exists (monitor_pipeline.py) but relies on manual invocation. The verification matrix is a strong sustainability signal — ensures new modules get tests. But: no integration tests for the full daily workflow, no smoke test for the automation chain, no canary for silent failures.
strengths: Verification matrix ensures new modules get tested
weaknesses: No alerting on launchd agent failures — failures are silent
No integration test for the daily automation chain
No canary mechanism to detect silent data corruption
